title: running podman system reset breaks podman machine
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If you have podman machines and run `podman system reset` it will only remove one part of the machine files.

The problem is that system reset will delete `~/.local/share/containers/` and some machine files are also stored there. I have no problem with removing them however since the machine config files are stored under `~/.config/containers/podman/machine` all machine commands now fail.

So either podman system reset should not delete machine files or it should delete all of them (must make sure to stop the vms before)


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init`

2. `podman system reset -f`

3. `podman machine ls`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: error listing vms: stat /home/pholzing/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-35.20220305.dev.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should work.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
latest main

comments.body: It should remove all of them and stop the machines.

I wanted to check this bug on my mac, but apparently `podman system reset` is not available on my installation of podman:

```sh
$ podman system --help
Manage podman

Description:
  Manage podman

Usage:
  podman system [command]

Available Commands:
  connection  Manage remote ssh destinations
  df          Show podman disk usage
  info        Display podman system information
  prune       Remove unused data
```

Am I missing something?
podman system reset is a local podman command only. It is not supported with the remote client.
Fixed in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14066, the PR did not link this issue.
